heading: Authority to assist mortgagors in default
label: 1715u Authority to assist mortgagors in default
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsecs. (c)(1)(A) and (f), was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , which is classified principally to this chapter (§ 1701 et seq.). For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.act June 27, 1934, ch. 84748 Stat. 1246

Amendments2009—Subsec. (a). –(E), inserted “preforeclosure sale, support for borrower housing counseling, subordinate lien resolution, borrower incentives,” after “loan modification,”, “as required,” after “deeds in lieu of foreclosure,”, and “or subsection (c),” before “as provided”.Pub. L. 111–22, § 203(d)(1)(C), which directed substitution of “loan” for “loss”, was executed by making the substitution before “modification” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 111–22, § 203(d)(1)(B), inserted “or imminent default, as defined by the Secretary” after “default”.Pub. L. 111–22, § 203(d)(1)(A)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary may establish a program for payment of a partial claim to a mortgagee that agrees to apply the claim amount to payment of a mortgage on a 1- to 4-family residence that is in default. Any such payment under such program to the mortgagee shall be made in the sole discretion of the Secretary and on terms and conditions acceptable to the Secretary, except that—Pub. L. 111–22, § 203(d)(2)“(1) the amount of the payment shall be in an amount determined by the Secretary, not to exceed an amount equivalent to 12 of the monthly mortgage payments and any costs related to the default that are approved by the Secretary; and“(2) the mortgagor shall agree to repay the amount of the insurance claim to the Secretary upon terms and conditions acceptable to the Secretary.The Secretary may pay the mortgagee, from the appropriate insurance fund, in connection with any activities that the mortgagee is required to undertake concerning repayment by the mortgagor of the amount owed to the Secretary.”Subsec. (a) to (e) as (b) to (f), respectively.Pub. L. 105–2761996— amended section generally, substituting subsecs. (a) to (e) relating to authority to assist mortgagors in default for former subsecs. (a) to (d) relating to temporary mortgage assistance payments and acquisition of mortgages to avoid foreclosures.Pub. L. 104–99Subsec. (d). struck out heading and text of subsec. (d). Text read as follows: “Any mortgage for which the mortgagor has applied to the Secretary, before the date of enactment of the Departments of Veterans Affairs and Housing and Urban Development, and Independent Agencies Appropriations Act, 1996, for assignment pursuant to subsection (b) of this section as in effect before such date of enactment shall continue to be governed by the provisions of this section, as in effect immediately before such date of enactment.”Pub. (a)(5). substituted “The interest rate on payments made under this subsection shall be the rate established under . The interest rate to be charged shall be determined when the Secretary approves assistance under this subsection” for “The Secretary may establish interest charges on payments made under this subsection; except that such charges shall not exceed a rate which is more than the maximum interest rate applicable with respect to level payment mortgages insured pursuant to at the time assistance under this section is approved by the Secretary.”Pub. L. 100–242section 1803(c) of title 38section 1709(b) of this title1983—Subsec. (d). struck out “, to the extent practicable,” after “Secretary shall”.Pub. L. 98–1811980—Subsec. (a). added subsec. (a). Existing undesignated provisions were designated as subsec. (b)(1).Pub. L. 96–399Subsec. (b). designated existing undes­ignated provision as par. (1), made changes in phraseology which included applicability of remedy provided by subsection (a) of this section and determinations made pursuant to such subsection, and added pars. (2) and (3).Pub. L. 96–399Subsecs. (c), (d). added subsecs. (c) and (d).Pub. L. 96–3991967— substituted “Secretary” for “Commissioner” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 90–191964— authorized the Commissioner to acquire the loan and security notwithstanding the fact that he has previously approved a request of the mortgagee for an extension of the time for curing the default and of the time for commencing foreclosure proceedings or for otherwise acquiring title to the mortgaged property or has approved a modification of the mortgage for the purpose of changing the amortization provisions by recasting the unpaid balance and substituted provisions for acquisition of the loan and security upon payment of the insurance benefits in an amount equal to the unpaid principal balance of the loan plus any unpaid mortgage interest plus reimbursement for such costs and attorney’s fees as the Commissioner finds were properly incurred in connection with the defaulted mortgage and its assignment to the Commissioner for former provision for such acquisition upon issuance to the mortgagee of debentures having a total face value equal to the unpaid principal balance of the loan plus any accrued interest.Pub. L. 88–560

children.heading: Loss mitigation
children.label: a Loss mitigation
children.text: Upon default or imminent default, as defined by the Secretary of any mortgage insured under this subchapter, mortgagees shall engage in loss mitigation actions for the purpose of providing an alternative to foreclosure (including but not limited to actions such as special forbearance, loan modification, preforeclosure sale, support for borrower housing counseling, subordinate lien resolution, borrower incentives, and deeds in lieu of foreclosure, as required, but not including assignment of mortgages to the Secretary under ) or subsection (c), as provided in regulations by the Secretary.1section 1710(a)(1)(A) of this title2So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1So in original. Probably should be “ or subsection (c)),”.2section 1710(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Establishment of program
children.label: 1 Establishment of program
children.text: The Secretary may establish a program for payment of a partial claim to a mortgagee that agrees to apply the claim amount to payment of a mortgage on a 1- to 4-family residence that is in default or faces imminent default, as defined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the payment shall be in an amount determined by the Secretary, not to exceed an amount equivalent to 30 percent of the unpaid principal balance of the mortgage and any costs that are approved by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the partial claim payment shall first be applied to any arrearage on the mortgage, and may also be applied to achieve principal reduction;

children.label: C
children.text: the mortgagor shall agree to repay the amount of the insurance claim to the Secretary upon terms and conditions acceptable to the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: the Secretary may permit compensation to the mortgagee for lost income on monthly payments, due to a reduction in the interest rate charged on the mortgage;

children.label: E
children.text: expenses related to the partial claim or modification may not be charged to the borrower;

children.label: F
children.text: loans may be modified to extend the term of the mortgage to a maximum of 40 years from the date of the modification; and

children.label: G
children.text: the Secretary may permit incentive payments to the mortgagee, on the borrower’s behalf, based on successful performance of a modified mortgage, which shall be used to reduce the amount of principal indebtedness.

children.heading: Payments in connection with certain activities
children.label: 3 Payments in connection with certain activities
children.text: The Secretary may pay the mortgagee, from the appropriate insurance fund, in connection with any activities that the mortgagee is required to undertake concerning repayment by the mortgagor of the amount owed to the Secretary.

children.heading: Program authority
children.label: A Program authority
children.text: The Secretary may establish a program for assignment to the Secretary, upon request of the mortgagee, of a mortgage on a 1- to 4-family residence insured under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the mortgage was in default or facing imminent default, as defined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: the mortgagee has modified the mortgage to cure the default and provide for mortgage payments within the reasonable ability of the mortgagor to pay, at interest rates not exceeding current market interest rates; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary arranges for servicing of the assigned mortgage by a mortgagee (which may include the assigning mortgagee) through procedures that the Secretary has determined to be in the best interests of the appropriate insurance fund.

children.heading: Payment of insurance benefits
children.label: C Payment of insurance benefits
children.text: Upon accepting assignment of a mortgage under this paragraph, the Secretary may pay insurance benefits to the mortgagee from the appropriate insurance fund, in an amount that the Secretary determines to be appropriate, not to exceed the amount necessary to compensate the mortgagee for the assignment and any losses and expenses resulting from the mortgage modification.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: A Authority
children.text: The Secretary may encourage loan modifications for eligible delinquent mortgages or mortgages facing imminent default, as defined by the Secretary, through the payment of insurance benefits and assignment of the mortgage to the Secretary and the subsequent modification of the terms of the mortgage according to a loan modification approved by the mortgagee.

children.heading: Payment of benefits and assignment
children.label: B Payment of benefits and assignment
children.text: In carrying out this paragraph, the Secretary may pay insurance benefits for a mortgage, in the amount determined in accordance with , without reduction for any amounts modified, but only upon the assignment, transfer, and delivery to the Secretary of all rights, interest, claims, evidence, and records with respect to the mortgage specified in clauses (i) through (iv) of .section 1710(a)(5) of this titlesection 1710(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: re-assign the mortgage to the mortgagee under terms and conditions as are agreed to by the mortgagee and the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: act as a Government National Mortgage Association issuer, or contract with an entity for such purpose, in order to pool the mortgage into a Government National Mortgage Association security; or

children.label: iii
children.text: re-sell the mortgage in accordance with any program that has been established for purchase by the Federal Government of mortgages insured under this subchapter, and the Secretary may coordinate standards for interest rate reductions available for loan modification with interest rates established for such purchase.

children.heading: Loan servicing
children.label: D Loan servicing
children.text: In carrying out this paragraph, the Secretary may require the existing servicer of a mortgage assigned to the Secretary to continue servicing the mortgage as an agent of the Secretary during the period that the Secretary acquires and holds the mortgage for the purpose of modifying the terms of the mortgage, provided that the Secretary compensates the existing servicer appropriately, as such compensation is determined by the Secretary consistent, to the maximum extent possible, with . If the mortgage is resold pursuant to subparagraph (C)(iii), the Secretary may provide for the existing servicer to continue to service the mortgage or may engage another entity to service the mortgage.section 1709(b) of this title

children.heading: Prohibition of judicial review
children.label: d Prohibition of judicial review
children.text: No decision by the Secretary to exercise or forego exercising any authority under this section shall be subject to judicial review.

children.heading: Repealed. , , , 1321–291; renumbered title I, , ,Pub. L. 104–134, title I, § 101(e) [title II, § 221(b)(2)]110 Stat. 1321–257Pub. L. 104–140, § 1(a)110 Stat. 1327Apr. 26, 1996May 2, 1996
children.label: e Repealed. , , , 1321–291; renumbered title I, , ,Pub. L. 104–134, title I, § 101(e) [title II, § 221(b)(2)]110 Stat. 1321–257Pub. L. 104–140, § 1(a)110 Stat. 1327Apr. 26, 1996May 2, 1996

children.heading: Applicability of other laws
children.label: f Applicability of other laws
children.text: No provision of this chapter, or any other law, shall be construed to require the Secretary to provide an alternative to foreclosure for mortgagees with mortgages on 1- to 4-family residences insured by the Secretary under this chapter, or to accept assignments of such mortgages.
